debug-analysis.summary: Odoo tickets are fetched by the poller ("Found X recently changed tickets") but nothing is sent to Linear. No logs appear after the fetch log. Root cause identified and fixed as of 2026-04-23.

root-cause.title: ODOO_BOT_USER_ID matches all human-edited tickets
root-cause.explanation:
The .env file sets ODOO_BOT_USER_ID=57. The middleware authenticates to Odoo as asfand@zumasales.com (ODOO_USERNAME), whose numeric Odoo UID is 57.
Every Odoo ticket stores write_uid = [last_editor_id, last_editor_name]. When a human edits a ticket using the asfand@zumasales.com account, write_uid[0] = 57.
The poller had a guard: if (ticket.write_uid[0] === env.ODOO_BOT_USER_ID) { continue; } ...logged at DEBUG level (invisible at INFO log level).
processOdooToLinear had the same guard: if (ticket.write_uid[0] === env.ODOO_BOT_USER_ID) { return; }
Because the middleware service account and the human test account are the SAME Odoo user (UID 57), EVERY ticket is silently discarded by both checks. The "Found X tickets" log fires (before the loop), then silence.

evidence.item: Bot-skip log was logger.debug — invisible at default INFO level
No "Enqueueing ticket" log ever appeared despite tickets being found

secondary-issue.title: Worker was never started (fixed earlier in session)
secondary-issue.explanation: src/index.ts did not import or call startWorker() from src/queue/worker.ts. The BullMQ Worker instance was never created, so even if jobs were enqueued they would sit in Redis indefinitely with no processor.
secondary-issue.fix: Added import and startWorker() call to src/index.ts.

section.name: Loop Prevention — Correct Design
section.description:
The middleware must prevent infinite sync loops: Odoo change → update Linear → Linear webhook → update Odoo → poll → update Linear → ...
The correct place to break this loop is in processLinearToOdoo, using the LINEAR_BOT_USER_ID check. When the middleware updates a Linear issue, Linear fires a webhook. The webhook actor is the middleware's API key user (LINEAR_BOT_USER_ID = 8c2da2c3-...). processLinearToOdoo detects this and returns early — Odoo is never written to again. Loop broken. ✓
The Odoo write_uid checks were an INCORRECT second loop-prevention attempt. They are redundant (the Linear bot check already stops the loop) and harmful (they filter legitimate human changes when the same account is used for both the middleware service and human editing).
section.loop-trace: 1. Human edits Odoo ticket (write_uid = 57) 2. Poller detects it → enqueues job [NOW FIXED: no write_uid filter] 3. processOdooToLinear runs → updates Linear issue 4. Linear fires webhook (actor = LINEAR_BOT_USER_ID) 5. processLinearToOdoo: actorId === LINEAR_BOT_USER_ID → returns early ✓ 6. Odoo is NOT written → no new poll event → loop stopped ✓

section.name: Why ODOO_BOT_USER_ID is still needed
section.description:
ODOO_BOT_USER_ID is no longer used for loop prevention (see above). It was removed from both the poller and processOdooToLinear. The env variable can remain for reference but currently has no effect.
LONG-TERM RECOMMENDATION: Create a dedicated Odoo service account for the middleware (separate from asfand@zumasales.com). This separates middleware writes from human writes cleanly, and ODOO_BOT_USER_ID can be re-introduced as a valid loop-prevention check once the accounts are separated.

file.change: Added import and startWorker() call so the BullMQ worker actually runs.
Improved shutdown handlers to close the worker cleanly.
Removed write_uid bot check from the polling loop entirely.
Added info-level logs before and after syncQueue.add() for traceability.
Removed write_uid bot check from processOdooToLinear.
Added info-level logs at each decision point (idempotency, mapping lookup, checksum match, conflict resolution) so every skip reason is visible in logs.
Added logs before and after Linear API create/update calls.
Improved job-start log to include data preview.
Added warning for unknown job names.

risk.title: Conflict resolution clock skew
risk.description: processOdooToLinear uses: const linearWins = mapping.updated_at > odooWriteDate; If the DB server and Odoo server clocks differ by more than a few seconds, "Linear wins" may be evaluated incorrectly, causing valid Odoo changes to be skipped. Monitor the conflict-resolution log line for unexpected "Linear wins" decisions.

risk.title: Database unreachable (Docker hostname)
risk.description: DATABASE_URL uses hostname "postgres" which is a Docker service name. If the app runs outside Docker while the DB is inside Docker, all Prisma calls will throw "connection refused" errors. Ensure the app and DB are in the same Docker network, or change DATABASE_URL to 127.0.0.1:5432.

risk.title: BullMQ duplicate job ID when previous job failed
risk.description: Job IDs are odoo-ticket-{id}-{write_date}. If a job fails all 5 retries and stays in the failed queue, subsequent polls for the same ticket with the same write_date will NOT create a new job (BullMQ deduplicates by ID). To reprocess a stuck ticket, manually remove the failed job from the queue, or modify the ticket in Odoo to change its write_date.
